subject: Statistics
title: The Normal Distribution
description: A fast breakdown of The Normal Distribution for builders.

hook.text: Heights, test scores, blood pressure — plot enough of them and the same shape appears. A bell curve.

buildup.text: The normal distribution is symmetric. Most values cluster near the mean, with fewer at the extremes.

discovery.text: The 68-95-99.7 rule: 68% of data falls within one standard deviation, 95% within two, 99.7% within three.

twist.text: Many things that look normal aren't. Income, city sizes, and follower counts all break the bell curve.

climax.text: The normal distribution is powerful but not universal. Knowing when it fits — and when it fails — is key.

punchline.text: The bell curve is everywhere. Until it isn't.

quiz.question: What does the 68-95-99.7 rule describe?
options: The percentage of data within 1, 2, and 3 standard deviations of the mean
The probability of three coin flips
How many outliers exist in any dataset
